sutta: the buddha said i will teach you these 9 attainments of gradual abiding listen pay heed i will speak and what monks are the 9 attainments where sense desires end and those who have ended since desires so abide surely those bearable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do sense desires end and who having ended since desires so abide i know not this i see not this who should speak thus to him this ought to be said consider reverend sir the monk who allude from sen's desires aloof from unwholesome states enters and abides in the first jhana which is accompanied by applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of seclusion their sense desires end and those who have ended sense desires so abide surely the guideless man without deceit would rejoice and be glad at this response and say well said and thereafter with join hands honor and reverence him just stop here for a moment so here the buddha buddha is talking actually about jhana states and states of concentration and in these states of concentration starting from the first jhana sense desires end and the buddha says that where sense desires and and those who have ended since desires so abide surely those valuable men craving not and cool nibuta have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond paragata this statement they have become cool nebuta and have crossed over and gone beyond paragata is normally used for the arahant [Music] supposed to have become enlightened and the buddha is using this for the jhana states which shows the great um respect and the great uh generation that the buddha puts in the jhana states states of concentration and then later the buddha goes on to discriminate the first jhana the state of the first ghana is um among allu from sen's desires allu from unwholesome states enters and abides in the first jhana so to be able to enter the first jhana you have to be aloof or secluded from sense desires aloof or secluded from unwholesome states which means that the first jhana is a statement which is totally wholesome holy wholesome because it is uh aloof from unwholesome states so here the buddha says that um if you after hearing uh this that the person who abides in the first jhana their sense desires and and the 1 who has ended since desires abides in that state then that person then anyone who hears that i should rejoice and be glad and should honor and reverence him and the buddha continues where thought applied and sustained and those who have ended such thoughts so abide surely those variable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the man who with the stilling of applied and sustained thought enters and abides in the second jhana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of concentration surely the godless man would rejoice at this response so stop here so in the state of the second jana the buddha says the person has to to still apply it and sustain thought applied and sustain thoughts and have to end before he can enter the second jana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought but with delight and pleasure born of concentration [Music] then the buddha continues where delight ends and those who have ended delight so abide surely those vulnerable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the monk who with the fading away of the life abides in equanimity and mindful and aware enters and abides in the third ghana and experiences pleasure with his body whereof the aryans declare he that is equanimous and mindful has a joyful abiding there the light ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the godless man would rejoice at this response and stop here again so here the state of the third jhana you see that delight has to fade away and among um when he enters in abides in the third jana he is said to be equanimous and mindful and has a joyful abiding so you see the state of the third jana is very clear that it is a mindful statement because some people they have a mistaken idea that jhanas are states of unmindfulness as though in a trance but states of jhana are in 10 states of mindfulness the mind is very clear and very strong and equanimous and because the mind is equanimous and that is why there is no tendency to lust for jhana unlike sensual pleasures sensual pleasures and there is delight but the mind is not equanimous and because of that the mind is very active there is a tendency to lust for that kind of pleasure but not with states of high concentration [Music] and then the buddha continues when equanimous pleasure ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those vulnerable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide considers the mong who with the abandoning of bodily pleasure and the previous abandoning of bodily pain by the fading away of joy and grief he enters and abides in the fourth jana which has complete purity of mindfulness and equanimity and has neither pain nor pleasure then equanimous pleasure ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the guideless man would rejoice at this response stop here again for a moment so in the state of the fourth jhana bodily pleasure and bodily pain and mental joy and mental grief have ended before that person can enter into the fourth jhana which is a state of complete purity of mindfulness and equanimity so here again now you see here that the fourth jana is the state of perfect mindfulness it is not an unmindful statement and the buddha continues where perceptions of form end and those who abide surely those variable men craving not and cool have but at very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the mong who unattentive to the perceptions of the many fall enters and abides in the sphere of infinite space their perceptions of form and and those who have ended them so abide surely the guideless man would rejoice at this response and stop here again for a moment so here the buddha is talking about the the first arupa jana which is the sphere of infinite space here the mind opens up to infinite space so that the mind becomes boundless in a way and in this state now perceptions of the many fold meaningful the bollywood is nanata and it also means diversity here the manga is unattentive he's not aware of perceptions of diversity that means he doesn't see all beings as individuals it doesn't consider all individual beings or as individuals and this is the beginning of the perception of no self the mind opens up and it refuses though all beings are 1 in his mind this is why china states are very important for insight for insider the higher wisdom because when a person comes out of those states the the insights are not the same as a person who uses the normal mind then the buddha continues where the perception of the sphere of infinite space ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those vulnerable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the monk who thinking consciousness is infinite enters and abides in the sphere of infinite consciousness there the perception of the sphere of infinite space ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the godless man would rejoice at this response where the perception of the sphere of infinite consciousness ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those vulnerable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the man who thinking there is nothing enters and abides in the sphere of nothingness there the perception of the sphere of infinite conscious consciousness ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the guideless man would rejoice at this response where the perception of the sphere of nothingness ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those verbal men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the monk who passing wholly beyond the sphere of nothingness enters and abides in the sphere of neither perception or non-perception there the perception of the sphere of nothingness ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the guideless man would rejoice at this response where the perception of the sphere of neither perception nor non-perception ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those verbal men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where ends the perception of this fear and who having ended it so abide i know not this i see not this who should speak thus to him this ought to be said consider reverend sir the man who passing wholly beyond that sphere enters and abides in the ending of perception and feeling there the perception of the sphere of neither perception nor non-perception ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the guideless man would without deceit would rejoice and be glad that this response and say well said and thereafter with join hands honor and reverence him verily monks these are the 9 attainments of gradual abiding that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so after that the buddha goes on to the arupa jana called the sphere of infinite consciousness in that state the mind the consciousness becomes infinite and a person who has this kind of statement he can know things that happen in far away places because the mind opens up and he has infinite consciousness and then this fear of nothingness in that state he realizes that everything is like mind made that there is nothing is all the whole universe is mind made exists in consciousness and basically there is nothing it is like imagination and then after that this fear of neither perception or non-perception where the mind is so uh still that it's almost the consciousness is almost about to be like a small flame about to to be blown off so sometimes that person is like and another time means as to why he doesn't perceive this why it's called the sphere of neither perception nor non-perception and the final state now is the state where perception and feeling ends when perception and feeling ends then the normal sex consciousness also ends and so these are the 9 states where the buddha praises now the next few sutas all have to do with states of ghana concentration the next 1 is
